investments.headline: Edinburgh spinout secures £10m to solve two energy crises at once
investments.summary: Exergy3, a University of Edinburgh spinout developing ultra-high temperature thermal energy storage, has raised £10m in seed funding led by Axeleo Capital. The company converts surplus renewable electricity into high-temperature heat for industrial use. Funds will be used to scale manufacturing, double headcount, and open a Munich office.
investments.raw_snippet: Exergy3 has raised £10 million in seed funding to scale its proprietary technology addressing three of Europe's most pressing challenges: industrial decarbonisation, grid balancing and energy security. Lead investor Guillaume Sarlat stated: 'we at AXC Greentech Industry are proud to lead this £10m seed round in support of their commercial rollout.'

investments.headline: Highway Data Systems secures £1.25 million investment from IFS managed by Maven
investments.summary: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS), a provider of automated roadside quality assurance technology, has secured £1.25m from the Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven. HDS is the first business to secure investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster via the IFS. Funds will support new contracts, customer expansion, and continued US operations development.
investments.raw_snippet: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS), a provider of automated roadside quality assurance, has secured a £1.25 million investment from the British Business Bank's Investment Fund for Scotland, through Fund Manager, Maven. HDS is the first business to secure investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster via the Investment Fund for Scotland.

investments.headline: Cnuic secures $3M pre-seed to unlock next-generation photonic chip production
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based Cnuic has raised $3m in an oversubscribed pre-seed round led by Tensor Ventures to develop a new type of photolithography device exploiting the wave properties of light. The technology enables rapid, reconfigurable production of photonic chips with enhanced 3D control. Investors described it as potentially giving Europe 'a whole new role in the semiconductor industry'.
investments.raw_snippet: Cnuic has raised $3 million in pre-seed funding led by Tensor Ventures, together with the Silicon Valley-based Blank Space Ventures. The round also saw participation from Silicon Roundabout Ventures, Phasechange, SANDS, and Superlative, and was oversubscribed. Martin Drdúl, co-founder of Tensor Ventures, characterizes this as 'a completely new technology and a major breakthrough that could mean a whole new role for Europe in the semiconductor industry'.

investments.headline: Quantcore raises £2.5m to build sovereign quantum supply chain
investments.summary: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spinout and the only UK company manufacturing niobium-based quantum components, raised £2.5m in seed funding co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise. The company supplies quantum processors, resonators, and sensors to UK national laboratories. Funding will support headcount growth from 4 to 12 employees over 18 months. A secondary source (FutureScot, 2026-04-01) reports a £250k STAC Invest tranche as part of a wider £772k accelerator round; this is assessed as part of the same seed raise given overlapping investors (STAC appears in both) and dates within 36 days.
investments.raw_snippet: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, has raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also coming from Quantum Exponential and STAC. The company manufactures niobium-based quantum components, operating from the James Watt Nanofabrication Centre.

investments.headline: University of Edinburgh biotech spin-out BIOCAPTIVA raises £1.58M
investments.summary: BIOCAPTIVA, a University of Edinburgh spinout, raised £1.58m led by Archangels to scale its novel magnetic bead technology for liquid biopsy cancer testing. The company's msX platform extracts DNA directly from whole blood without centrifuges, making cancer diagnostics faster and more accessible. The raise coincides with the launch of its first product in the US market.
investments.raw_snippet: University of Edinburgh spin-out BIOCAPTIVA has raised £1.58 million in a new funding round and launched its first product in the US. The raise was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise, and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners. BIOCAPTIVA's novel magnetic bead technology is designed to solve one of liquid biopsy's biggest bottlenecks: preparing blood samples for cancer research and diagnostics.

investments.headline: Funding round worth £200k to help Scottish startup make Edinburgh most flexible working capital
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based remote working platform SWURF raised £200k from named angel investors including Gareth Williams (Skyscanner co-founder) and hospitality entrepreneur Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson. The investment will fund rollout of SWURF Pods — private on-demand meeting spaces — across Edinburgh. This follows a six-figure raise in 2025.
investments.raw_snippet: Edinburgh-based remote working platform SWURF has announced a new £200,000 funding round. The investment will accelerate SWURF's expansion, with the goal of turning Edinburgh into the world's most flexible working friendly city. Existing backers include Gareth Williams, co-founder of Skyscanner, and hospitality entrepreneur Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson.

investments.headline: Ultrasonic surgery specialist Nami appoints Nikki Palfrey as CEO and completes latest investment round
investments.summary: Nami Surgical, a University of Glasgow spinout developing ultrasonic robotic-assisted surgery technology, completed a £1.9m round led by Eos Advisory with participation from Maven and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation in the robotic-assisted surgery market. New CEO Nikki Palfrey was appointed alongside the close.
investments.raw_snippet: Nami recently completed its latest investment, a £1.9 million round led by Eos Advisory with participation from the Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven Capital Partners (and delivered by the British Business Bank), and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation as Nami targets the global robotic-assisted surgery market, which is forecast to reach over $25 billion by 2030.

investments.headline: Maven backs Esk with £2.6m investment to support international growth
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based live entertainment technology company Esk has secured £2.6m from Maven Capital Partners and the Investment Fund for Scotland. Esk specialises in licensing iconic IP and producing live experiences for brands including Netflix, Paramount and BAFTA. The funding will support international expansion, marketing, and development of its proprietary hardware.
investments.raw_snippet: Maven has completed an investment in Esk, a global entertainment company which delivers high-end live experiences for blue chip brands such as Netflix, Paramount and BAFTA. The company specializes in licensing intellectual property and transforming it into live entertainment formats. CEO Sam Weatherstone said: 'This investment from Maven and the British Business Bank is a massive vote of confidence in the company and our approach.'

investments.headline: Three Glasgow 'deeptech' companies complete £772,000 investment round
investments.summary: Vuabl, a Glasgow proptech startup using smartphone LIDAR sensors to create 3D property models and assess energy performance, raised £222k from STAC Invest. The company is running a pilot with Glasgow's Wheatley Group covering 65,000 homes. Funding will support further pilots and expansion to housing providers facing new regulatory obligations.
investments.raw_snippet: Three Glasgow 'deeptech' companies spanning quantum computing, housing management and offshore wind infrastructure have completed a £772,000 investment round. Vuabl uses the LIDAR sensors found in modern phones to create 3D models, estimate energy performance and flag maintenance issues — tools intended to help landlords and social housing providers meet tightening regulatory requirements.

investments.headline: Three Glasgow 'deeptech' companies complete £772,000 investment round
investments.summary: Airspection, a Glasgow deeptech company focused on offshore wind infrastructure inspection, raised £300k from STAC Invest. The company completed STAC's 18-month accelerator programme and established its first headquarters at thebeyond, STAC's technology hub at Skypark in Glasgow.
investments.raw_snippet: Three Glasgow 'deeptech' companies spanning quantum computing, housing management and offshore wind infrastructure have completed a £772,000 investment round. Airspection and Vuabl establishing their first HQ's at thebeyond, STAC's 22,000 sq ft technology hub at Skypark in Glasgow.
